channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Panama president: 'Impossible' to negotiate over canal
item.description: Panama’s President José Raúl Mulino rejected negotiations over ownership of the Panama Canal, in the face of President Trump’s fixation on the U.S. retaking the trade waterway.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Expected release of high-profile male hostages sparks excitement and anxiety in Israel
item.description: News that three high-profile hostages are expected to be released has brought excitement and trepidation to Israel on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Julia Frankel, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Russia claims another Ukrainian village has fallen as it closes in on a key city
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia on Friday claimed it had captured another village in its relentless offensive in eastern Ukraine’s Donetsk region and as it closes in on the critical Ukrainian logistics hub of Pokrovsk after almost three years of war.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Serbian student protesters march ahead of bridge blockade as driver rams Belgrade demonstration
item.description: Hundreds of striking students are marching through the Serbian countryside, taking their anti-graft protest toward the northern city of Novi Sad, where they plan to blockade three bridges over the River Danube this weekend.
item.dc:creator: Eldar Emric, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Tens of thousands of Muslims attend annual Biswa Ijtema event in Bangladesh
item.description: Tens of thousands of people have gathered on a riverbank near Bangladesh’s capital to listen to sermons by Islamic scholars at the Biswa Ijtema or global congregation of Muslim devotees.
item.dc:creator: Julhas Alam, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Middle East latest: Families welcome news of 3 more male hostages to be released
item.description: The organization representing families of hostages held in the Gaza strip is celebrating the news that three male hostages would be released Saturday as part of the ceasefire agreement between Israel and Hamas.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Sacred strokes of color on foreheads are a major display of Hinduism at India’s Maha Kumbh festival
item.description: PRAYAGRAJ, India (AP) — The pilgrims come and go as strangers. They march like a sea of people, walking toward the spot where they take holy baths, drawn by the hope that the bathing would bring them salvation.
item.dc:creator: SHEIKH SAALIQ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A French journalist recounts 711 days of captivity as a hostage of Islamic extremists in Mali
item.description: DAKAR, Senegal (AP) — For a foreign correspondent in Mali, the assignment seemed like a dream: as Islamic extremists upended the region, Olivier Dubois, a French journalist, secured a rare interview with a leader of JNIM, an al-Qaida’s affiliate in the Sahel. Or so he thought.
item.dc:creator: MONIKA PRONCZUK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israelis and Palestinians rejoice after more hostages and prisoners are freed
item.description: KHAN YOUNIS, Gaza Strip (AP) — Eight more hostages were freed from the Gaza Strip by Hamas-led militants on Thursday in a sometimes chaotic process that briefly delayed Israel's release of 110 Palestinian prisoners and underscored the fragility of the ceasefire that began earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: MOHAMMAD JAHJOUH, WAFAA SHURAFA and ISABEL DeBRE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Panama's president says there will be no negotiation about ownership of canal
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — Panama President José Raúl Mulino said Thursday there will be no negotiation with the United States over ownership of the Panama Canal, and he hopes U.S.
item.dc:creator: ALMA SOLÍS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate Democrats revolt against Trump in fallout from foreign aid freeze
item.description: Senate Democrats are starting to revolt against President Trump’s agenda on Capitol Hill amid fallout from an initial freeze on all foreign assistance, warning Republicans that failure to work together will incur consequences. Sen. Chris Coons (D-Del.) on Thursday reversed support for Rep.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Moment IDF soldier is reunited with family seen on video
item.description: Agam Berger, an IDF soldier who was taken hostage during Hamas' attacks on Oct. 7, 2023, is reunited with her family after spending 482 days in captivity.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: More schools in France are getting special mailboxes for children to flag abuse
item.description: PARIS (AP) — A special mailbox for children to flag abuses was set up Thursday at a Paris primary school, a first in the French capital as part of nationwide effort to encourage more victims of sexual violence to speak up.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Third round of hostage releases begins as part of Hamas' Gaza ceasefire agreement with Israel
item.description: Hamas released eight more hostages in Gaza Thursday as part of an ongoing ceasefire agreement with Israel in exchange for releasing hundreds of Palestinian prisoners.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A New Zealand mountain is granted personhood, recognizing it as sacred for Māori
item.description: A mountain in New Zealand considered an ancestor by Indigenous people has been recognized as a legal person after a new law granted it all the rights and responsibilities of a human being.
item.dc:creator: Charlotte Graham-Mclay, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Senate Republicans urge Trump to sanction ICC after failed legislation push
item.description: Senate Republicans are recommending President Trump issue an executive order to sanction the International Criminal Court (ICC) for its pursuit of war crimes cases against Israel, after Senate Democrats blocked sanctions legislation absent a bipartisan compromise.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Rubio exempting 'life-saving humanitarian assistance’ from foreign aid restrictions
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio issued a waiver on Tuesday exempting “life-saving humanitarian assistance” from President Trump’s executive order pausing nearly all foreign aid during a period of review. Trump’s executive order pausing funding, issued on his first day in office, suspended any new U.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Passenger plane catches fire at South Korean airport; all 176 people on board are evacuated
item.description: A passenger plane operated by Air Busan caught fire before takeoff at an airport in South Korea; all 176 people on board were safely evacuated using an escape slide.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former Hamas hostage details horrors of captivity, credits kidnapped IDF soldier with saving her life
item.description: Former hostage Amit Soussana, who was the first Israeli woman to speak about being sexual assaulted while in Hamas captivity, says kidnapped IDF soldier Liri Albag saved her life.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Year of the Snake is underway with Lunar New Year festivities around the world
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — Firecrackers popped, incense was offered at temples and dancers and drummers paraded Wednesday in Asia and farther afield as millions around the world celebrated the Lunar New Year.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Netanyahu accepts Trump’s invite, will meet at White House next week
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and President Trump are expected to meet next week in Washington, making him the first foreign leader to visit to the White House since Trump returned to office. The White House confirmed Netanyahu had accepted the invite for the Feb. 4 meeting.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: India's Modi speaks with 'dear friend' President Trump amid hopes of furthering ties
item.description: Following a call between President Donald Trump and Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi, India is seeking to boost bilateral ties and trade with the U.S. amid an already friendly relationship between the two leaders.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 85 percent of Greenlanders don't want to be part of US: Survey
item.description: A large majority of residents in Greenland are against President Trump's wish to acquire the island territory, according to a survey published Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: North Korean leader Kim inspects nuclear facility as Pyongyang pressures Trump administration
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korean leader Kim Jong Un inspected a facility that produces nuclear material and called for bolstering the country’s nuclear fighting capability, state media reported Wednesday, as the North looks to increase pressure on the United States following the inauguration o
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and FOSTER KLUG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Denmark to increase military spending in North Atlantic by $2 billion amid row with Trump over Greenland
item.description: Denmark is increasing military spending in the North Atlantic amid President Donald Trump’s bid to have Greenland sold or ceded to the United States.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli President Herzog highlights antisemitism in UN speech as new report shows shocking trend
item.description: Israel's President Isaac Herzog addressed the U.N. on Monday at its annual Holocaust Remembrance Day event. His speech comes as a new report shows a worrying rise in antisemitic attitudes globally.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A weakened Iran has Iraq looking to curb Tehran-backed extremists in country
item.description: The Iraqi government is looking to gain control of Iranian-aligned armed groups that operate inside of Iraq but outside state control, in yet another sign of Iran's weakened position since October 7.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: EU military chief says it would make sense to put European troops in Greenland, Welt reports
item.description: Top-ranking European military official Robert Brieger reportedly said it would "make perfect sense" to station troops in Greenland amid President Trump's interest in acquiring it.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Preliminary report on South Korean plane crash confirms bird strike, but no cause yet
item.description: The preliminary accident report released by South Korea's Aviation and Railway Accident Investigation Board on Monday said that feathers and bird blood stains were found in both engines.
item.dc:creator: Hyung-jin Kim, WGN 9

item.title: Belarus' President Lukashenko extends rule after election rejected by opposition, EU
item.description: Belarus held an orchestrated election over the weekend that the opposition and the EU rejected as a farce, extending President Lukashenko's more than 30 years in power.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: White House: Colombia to take deported migrants after Trump showdown
item.description: The White House said a U.S.-Colombia agreement had come together in the wake of a back-and-forth over immigration and tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Colombian president orders increase of import tariffs on US goods after Trump order
item.description: Colombian President Gustavo Petro, in a post on the social platform X, said he ordered the "foreign trade minister to raise import tariffs from the U.S. by 25%.
item.dc:creator: Elvia Limon, NewsNation

item.title: A child's life in Gaza, as explained by a Middle East reporter
item.description: Middle East reporter Arwa Damon, who founded the nonprofit INARA, described what she's seen in Gaza to NewsNation.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: CIA believes COVID most likely originated from lab but has low confidence in its own finding
item.description: The CIA now believes the virus responsible for the COVID-19 pandemic most likely originated from a laboratory, according to an assessment released Saturday that points the finger at China even while acknowledging that the spy agency has “low confidence” in its own conclusion.
item.dc:creator: David Klepper, WFLA, NBC
